message:
feat(messaging): delta-CRDT metadata layer for non-authoritative control surfaces

Add a composable delta-CRDT framework for control-plane metadata that
must converge across replicas without requiring authoritative fencing:
aggregated interest counts, coarse cursor checkpoints, membership
hints, load sketches, and advisory summaries.

Core framework (+~1000 lines):
- JoinSemilattice trait with Delta associated type, merge/delta/apply_delta
- ReplicaVersionVector: per-replica version tracking with dominance checks
- AntiEntropyDigest: sparse frontier summary for convergence protocol
- PropagationEnvelope: versioned delta payload with propagation mode
  (AntiEntropy/Eager/Snapshot)
- PropagationApply: Applied/AlreadyConverged/StaleIgnored outcomes
- CrdtPropagationReplica<T>: full convergence protocol driver with
  mutate(), record_local_state(), prepare_for(), apply()

Concrete CRDT types:
- InterestSummary: per-replica subject interest with PN-counter semantics
  (subscribe/unsubscribe/interest_count), JoinSemilattice impl
- CursorCheckpoint: per-cursor offset + timestamp marks with
  per-steward tracking, JoinSemilattice impl
- LagSketch: per-cursor consumer lag estimates (offset_lag + age_ms),
  JoinSemilattice impl with max-merge semantics

Tests (46 tests):
- Version vector advance/observe/merge/dominance
- InterestSummary subscribe/unsubscribe/merge/delta round-trips
- CursorCheckpoint checkpoint/merge/delta convergence
- LagSketch record/merge/delta/stale-age semantics
- CrdtPropagationReplica full convergence protocol tests:
  mutate→propagate→apply, anti-entropy digest exchange,
  stale envelope rejection, snapshot mode
- Two-replica convergence: interest, checkpoint, lag

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
